package.brief: HH Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The meeting reviewed bilateral relations and explored ways to expand cooperation in various fields. Regional developments in the Middle East were also discussed, underscoring the strong ties between Qatar and Pakistan.

article.headline: HH the Amir Receives Pakistani Prime Minister at Amiri Diwan
article.body:
HH Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday.

The meeting reviewed the strong bilateral relations between the two countries and explored ways to enhance cooperation in various fields, according to officials.

During the talks, HH the Amir emphasised the importance of deepening economic cooperation between Qatar and Pakistan, stating: "We value Pakistan's stance and look forward to deeper economic cooperation between our two countries."

The two sides also discussed regional developments in the Middle East, with both leaders reaffirming their commitment to stability and peace in the region.

The visit reflects the longstanding friendship and strategic partnership between Qatar and Pakistan, which have consistently worked together on mutual interests.

The meeting was attended by senior officials from both countries, including ministers and advisors.

This engagement is part of ongoing high-level diplomatic exchanges between Qatar and Pakistan, aimed at strengthening ties and exploring new avenues for collaboration.

The discussions are expected to lead to further cooperation in trade, investment, energy, and other sectors, benefiting both nations.

Qatar and Pakistan share a history of close relations, with regular visits and consultations at the highest levels.

The visit of the Pakistani Prime Minister underscores the importance both countries place on their partnership and their shared vision for regional peace and prosperity.

package.instagram_caption: HH Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan. The leaders discussed bilateral relations and regional developments. #Qatar #Pakistan #Diplomacy #AmiriDiwan #ShehbazSharif #TamimAlThani
package.story_copy: HH the Amir meets Pakistani PM at Amiri Diwan to boost ties.
package.push_notification: HH the Amir receives Pakistani PM at Amiri Diwan, discusses cooperation and regional stability.
package.video_script:

package.brief: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art has opened a new exhibition titled 'Resolutions' to mark its 15th anniversary. The exhibition explores the museum's foundation through four key areas, including its origins under Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, its exhibition history, engagement with knowledge circulation in the Arab world, and educational programmes. Entry is free for all visitors, highlighting the museum's commitment to accessibility.

article.headline: Mathaf Opens 'Resolutions' Exhibition Marking 15 Years of the Museum
article.body:
Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art has opened a new exhibition titled 'Resolutions' to commemorate its 15th anniversary, according to officials.

The exhibition examines Mathaf's foundation through four defining areas: its origins under Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, its exhibition history, its engagement with knowledge circulation in the Arab world, and its educational programmes.

'Resolutions' offers visitors a comprehensive look at the museum's journey since its establishment, highlighting key milestones and contributions to the art scene in Qatar and the region.

The exhibition is part of Mathaf's ongoing efforts to document and present modern and contemporary Arab art, fostering cultural dialogue and appreciation.

Entry to the exhibition is free for all visitors, in line with the museum's commitment to making art accessible to the community.

Mathaf, located in Doha's Education City, has been a cornerstone of Qatar's cultural landscape, hosting numerous exhibitions and educational initiatives over the past 15 years.

The museum's collection, one of the most significant of its kind in the region, includes works by prominent Arab artists from the 19th century to the present.

'Resolutions' runs until a date to be announced, and visitors are encouraged to check Mathaf's website for updated timings and related programmes.

The exhibition underscores Qatar's dedication to nurturing the arts and preserving cultural heritage, in line with the Qatar National Vision 2030.

package.instagram_caption: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art celebrates 15 years with the opening of 'Resolutions' exhibition. Explore the museum's origins, exhibition history, and educational programmes. Entry is free for all visitors. #Mathaf #QatarMuseums #ArtExhibition #DohaCulture #ModernArt #QatarLiving

package.brief: The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has launched a summer mosquito-control programme using drones and biological larvicide across 18 districts, coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality, running through October. The initiative aims to reduce mosquito-borne diseases during peak season, with officials emphasizing shared public-health responsibility.

article.headline: Ministry of Public Health Launches Summer Mosquito-Control Programme Using Drones and Biological Larvicide
article.body:
The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has announced the launch of a comprehensive summer mosquito-control programme, deploying drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts in Qatar. The initiative, coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality, will run through October to curb mosquito populations during the peak breeding season.

According to officials, the programme targets areas with high mosquito activity, using drone technology to survey and treat hard-to-reach locations. Biological larvicide, which relies on natural fish species, is being introduced in water bodies to prevent mosquito larvae from developing.

"Public-health vigilance during summer is a shared responsibility," said the MoPH Director of Communicable Diseases in a statement. The official urged residents to eliminate standing water around homes and report breeding sites to authorities.

The programme builds on Qatar's ongoing efforts to control vector-borne diseases such as dengue fever and malaria. By integrating modern technology with environmentally friendly methods, the MoPH aims to reduce reliance on chemical pesticides.

Residents in the 18 districts are advised to cooperate with field teams and take preventive measures. The Ministry of Municipality is supporting the programme through regular inspections and public awareness campaigns.

This initiative underscores Qatar's commitment to proactive public health management, ensuring a safe and healthy environment for all residents during the summer months.

package.instagram_caption: The Ministry of Public Health has launched a summer mosquito-control programme using drones and biological larvicide across 18 districts. The initiative, in coordination with the Ministry of Municipality, runs through October. Residents are urged to eliminate standing water and report breeding sites. #Qatar #PublicHealth #MosquitoControl #SummerSafety #MoPH #Doha

package.brief: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concluded on 24 May, and editorial operations are updating its status from scheduled to completed. The event brought together education leaders and stakeholders to discuss leadership in education. This update ensures the events page reflects accurate information for users.

article.headline: Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 Concludes, Status Updated to Completed
article.body:
The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concluded on 24 May, marking a successful gathering of education leaders and stakeholders. The event, which focused on leadership in education, featured discussions and networking opportunities.

According to organizers, the expo aimed to foster collaboration and innovation in the education sector. Attendees included representatives from schools, universities, and government entities.

Editorial operations at Qatar Living have now updated the event's status from EventScheduled to Completed on the live events page. This ensures that users see accurate information when browsing past events.

The expo is part of Qatar's ongoing efforts to enhance educational leadership and align with national development goals. Such events contribute to the exchange of ideas and best practices in education.

For more information on past and upcoming events, visit the Qatar Living events page.

package.brief: Qatar Airways will resume direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, starting 15 July. The expansion strengthens connectivity to key markets and brings the airline's global network to over 160 destinations this summer. The move supports Qatar's aviation hub ambitions and offers travelers more options.

article.headline: Qatar Airways to Resume Direct Flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from July 15
article.body:
Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, effective 15 July, further expanding its global network to over 160 destinations this summer.

The Helsinki route will operate four times weekly from 15 July, increasing to a daily service from 1 August. Tokyo Haneda will receive four weekly flights starting 15 July, complementing the existing daily service to Tokyo Narita.

"We are pleased to restore connectivity with Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda — two strategically important markets for our network," said Qatar Airways Chief Commercial Officer in a statement. The airline highlighted that the resumption reflects growing demand and its commitment to providing seamless travel options.

Helsinki, the capital of Finland, serves as a key gateway to Scandinavia and the Baltic region. Tokyo Haneda, located closer to central Tokyo than Narita, offers greater convenience for business and leisure travelers.

The expansion is part of Qatar Airways' ongoing strategy to rebuild and enhance its route network post-pandemic. The airline now serves over 160 destinations, reinforcing its position as one of the world's leading carriers.

Passengers can book flights via the Qatar Airways website or mobile app. The airline continues to implement health and safety measures in line with international guidelines.

This development supports Qatar's National Vision 2030 by boosting tourism and trade links, and solidifies Hamad International Airport's role as a global aviation hub.

package.instagram_caption: Qatar Airways is expanding its network! From 15 July, direct flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda resume. Helsinki will operate 4x weekly, increasing to daily from August. Tokyo Haneda joins existing Narita service with 4 weekly flights. Book now! #QatarAirways #Helsinki #TokyoHaneda #Travel #Aviation
package.story_copy: Qatar Airways resumes flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from 15 July, expanding network to over 160 destinations.
